scope_note: QA Witt Tower Cross-Domain Regime Discriminator cert [442]. Empirical demonstration that the Witt tower filter bank (cert [439]) discriminates physical and financial market activity regimes via fixed-layer and birth-layer occupation fractions, validated across two statistically independent domains. COMPANION: M=[[5,-1],[1,0]], p=3, r=1 (det=+1, cert [439]), tower level k=3 (mod 27). DOMAIN 1 — SILSO Monthly Sunspot Number (Royal Observatory of Belgium, 1749-present): Encoding b=int(SN[t]) mod 27, e=int(SN[t-1]) mod 27 (direct observer projection; no feedback); solar_min (SN<20): fixed-layer 7.9%, solar_max (SN>100): fixed-layer 0.3%; absolute delta 7.7pp; permutation test p<0.001 (N=200). DOMAIN 2 — S&P 500 Monthly Log-Returns (Yahoo Finance ^GSPC, ~1982-2026; hardcoded 2000-2024 fallback): Encoding rank-normalized log-returns to Z/27Z, state=(rank[t], rank[t-1]); NBER recession months (2001-03/11, 2007-12/2009-06, 2020-02/04): fixed-layer 9.7%; expansion: fixed-layer 0.9%; absolute delta 8.8pp; permutation test p=0.015 (N=200). CROSS-DOMAIN: both domains show overall birth fraction 65.8-66.1% (theory 2/3=66.7%) and large fixed-layer regime differential (7-9pp), consistent across encoding methods (direct-mod for SILSO, rank-normalized for S&P). CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) SILSO birth fraction 66.1% in [55%,75%]. (C2) SILSO |delta_fixed| = 7.7pp >= 4pp. (C3) SILSO permutation p < 0.15. (C4) S&P 500 birth fraction 65.8% in [55%,75%]. (C5) S&P 500 |delta_fixed| = 8.8pp >= 4pp. (C6) S&P 500 permutation p < 0.15. (C7) Both domains satisfy all three gates simultaneously. Theorem NT compliance: time-series values->integer states are one-way observer projections; filter bank output never re-enters QA layer as causal input. PRIMARY SOURCES: Clette, F. et al. (2015) J. Space Weather Space Clim. 5 A9 doi:10.5194/jswsc-5-A9-2015 (SILSO dataset); NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee www.nber.org/cycles (recession reference dates); Wall, D.D. (1960) Amer. Math. Monthly 67(6) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano lifting law underlying cert [439]). Derived and validated 2026-06-17.
